source_paper_title: Convergence of Sinkhorn’s Algorithm for Entropic Martingale Optimal Transport Problem
context:
Section 4.1, Remark 4.2 (p. 23)

For the case of high dimensional EMOT problem when \(X,Y = \mathbb R^d\), in order to mimic the argument of the Theorem 2.10, we need but failed to construct a \(\hat\pi \sim \mu\otimes\nu\otimes\rho\) such that \(H(\hat\pi\mid\mu\otimes\nu\otimes\rho) < +\infty\) and \(\int_{Y\times Z}(y-x)\,\hat\pi_{y,z\mid x}(dy,dz) = \epsilon(x)\,x/\|x\|\) for \(x\in X\), \(\mu\)-a.s, where \(\epsilon(x)\) has a uniform positive lower bound \(\epsilon_0\), i.e., \(\epsilon(x) \ge \epsilon_0\).
title: Construct an absolutely continuous perturbation coupling \,\(\hat\pi\) with uniformly outward drift in high-dimensional EMOT
background_latex:
Let \(d\in\mathbb{N}_+\). Let \(\mu,
u\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R^d)\) be probability measures and let \(ho\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R)\) be a reference measure on an auxiliary space \(Z=\mathbb R\). Let \(\mu\otimes
u\otimesho\) denote the product measure on \(\mathbb R^d	imes\mathbb R^d	imes\mathbb R\). For a probability measure \(\hat\pi\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R^d	imes\mathbb R^d	imes\mathbb R)\), write \(\hat\pi\sim \mu\otimes
u\otimesho\) to mean mutual absolute continuity. Let \(H(\hat\pi\mid \mu\otimes
u\otimesho)\) be the relative entropy.

Let \(X,Y,Z\) denote the coordinate projections under \(\hat\pi\). Assume \(\hat\pi\) has \(X\)-marginal \(\mu\); write \(\hat\pi_{Y,Z\mid X=x}\) for a regular conditional distribution of \((Y,Z)\) given \(X=x\). For \(x\in\mathbb R^d\setminus\{0\}\), let \(x/\|x\|\) be the corresponding unit vector (Euclidean norm \(\|\cdot\|\)). Let \(\epsilon:\mathbb R^d	o\mathbb R\) be a measurable function and let \(\epsilon_0>0\) be a constant.
problem_definition_latex:
Construct (under appropriate, explicitly stated assumptions on \(\mu,\nu,\rho\)) a probability measure \(\hat\pi\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R^d\times\mathbb R^d\times\mathbb R)\) such that:
- \(\hat\pi\sim \mu\otimes\nu\otimes\rho\) and \(H(\hat\pi\mid \mu\otimes\nu\otimes\rho)<+\infty\).
- The \(X\)-marginal of \(\hat\pi\) equals \(\mu\).
- For \(\mu\)-a.e. \(x\in\mathbb R^d\setminus\{0\}\), the conditional increment satisfies
\[\int_{\mathbb R^d\times\mathbb R} (y-x)\,\hat\pi_{Y,Z\mid X=x}(dy,dz)=\epsilon(x)\,\frac{x}{\|x\|},\]
with a uniform positivity bound \(\epsilon(x)\ge \epsilon_0\) for \(\mu\)-a.e. \(x\).
latex:
Let \(d\in\mathbb{N}_+\). Let \(\mu,\nu\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R^d)\) be probability measures and let \(\rho\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R)\) be a reference measure on an auxiliary space \(Z=\mathbb R\). Let \(\mu\otimes\nu\otimes\rho\) denote the product measure on \(\mathbb R^d\times\mathbb R^d\times\mathbb R\). For a probability measure \(\hat\pi\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R^d\times\mathbb R^d\times\mathbb R)\), write \(\hat\pi\sim \mu\otimes\nu\otimes\rho\) to mean mutual absolute continuity. Let \(H(\hat\pi\mid \mu\otimes\nu\otimes\rho)\) be the relative entropy.

Let \(X,Y,Z\) denote the coordinate projections under \(\hat\pi\). Assume \(\hat\pi\) has \(X\)-marginal \(\mu\); write \(\hat\pi_{Y,Z\mid X=x}\) for a regular conditional distribution of \((Y,Z)\) given \(X=x\). For \(x\in\mathbb R^d\setminus\{0\}\), let \(x/\|x\|\) be the corresponding unit vector (Euclidean norm \(\|\cdot\|\)). Let \(\epsilon:\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R\) be a measurable function and let \(\epsilon_0>0\) be a constant.

Construct (under appropriate, explicitly stated assumptions on \(\mu,\nu,\rho\)) a probability measure \(\hat\pi\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R^d\times\mathbb R^d\times\mathbb R)\) such that:
- \(\hat\pi\sim \mu\otimes\nu\otimes\rho\) and \(H(\hat\pi\mid \mu\otimes\nu\otimes\rho)<+\infty\).
- The \(X\)-marginal of \(\hat\pi\) equals \(\mu\).
- For \(\mu\)-a.e. \(x\in\mathbb R^d\setminus\{0\}\), the conditional increment satisfies
\[\int_{\mathbb R^d\times\mathbb R} (y-x)\,\hat\pi_{Y,Z\mid X=x}(dy,dz)=\epsilon(x)\,\frac{x}{\|x\|},\]
with a uniform positivity bound \(\epsilon(x)\ge \epsilon_0\) for \(\mu\)-a.e. \(x\).
open_problem_quote: For the case of high dimensional EMOT problem when \(X,Y = \mathbb R^d\), in order to mimic the argument of the Theorem 2.10, we need but failed to construct a \(\hat\pi \sim \mu\otimes\nu\otimes\rho\) such that \(H(\hat\pi\mid\mu\otimes\nu\otimes\rho) < +\infty\) and \(\int_{Y\times Z}(y-x)\,\hat\pi_{y,z\mid x}(dy,dz) = \epsilon(x)\,x/\|x\|\) for \(x\in X\), \(\mu\)-a.s, where \(\epsilon(x)\) has a uniform positive lower bound \(\epsilon_0\), i.e., \(\epsilon(x) \ge \epsilon_0\).
